question.text: A trader sells (writes) a USDINR call with strike 84.00 at Rs. 0.20 premium. At expiry, USDINR is 84.75. What is the writer's net P&L per unit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Writer payoff = Premium - max(S-K, 0) = 0.20 - 0.75 = -Rs. 0.55 loss. **The Trap:** Rs. 0.75 ignores premium received. Writer keeps premium which offsets part of loss. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short call P&L is mirror of long call - tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Per lot, writer loss = Rs. 550. Maximum profit = premium = Rs. 200 per lot.
